what_it_does: This file is a staged, machine-readable synthesis of 207 cross-cutting emergent findings from eight source collections, clustered into 28 named rule clusters and classified against live DNA faces as covered, new, extended, or conflicting, with target face paths, rationales, provenance counts, computed formulas, proposed new sections, and open questions for Tim.
observations: 28 named clusters, 15 augmentations (4 new, 10 extends, 1 conflict), 13 covered-no-action items, 3 proposed new sections, and 6 open questions.
The highest-recurrence gap is per-role character budgets plus a never-wrap law and sibling length-calibration, recurring in all 8 source collections.
It proposes a new DNA face, dna/data-viz.json, to own warm-only chart and map colour rules.
It records live-DNA conflicts explicitly rather than resolving them, including octagon identity, compression models, future encodings, and green/red semantics.
It distinguishes covered-as-principle from gap-as-concrete-encoding as a central classification grain.
Computed rules are written as formulas intended for derivation, e.g., col_width, content_budget, opacity, chart_series.
Provenance is expressed as recurrence counts across eight named source collections.
standouts: Character-budget gap flagged as the #1 leverage point across the whole corpus.
Warm-only data-viz palette elevated to a dedicated new face.
Three competing encodings of future surfaced for Tim to rule on.
Compression models conflict: progressive apparatus-drop versus slide-as-atom omission.
Status-dot molecule is the one place non-directional green is proposed.
conventions: Each augmentation uses target, name, value_or_recipe, rationale, provenance, status, conflict_note.
Status vocabulary: new, extends-existing, conflicts-with-existing, covered.
Recurrence count out of 8 source collections is used as a proxy for rule strength.
References to live DNA use exact key paths like dna/voice.json > forms.*.
Rules are expressed as derivable predicates or formulas rather than hand-placed values.
concerns: Several high-leverage rules rest on low recurrence (2/8), e.g., opacity-as-temporal and status-dot.
Status calls require knowledge of live DNA faces not present in this file.
Multiple unresolved conflicts are left as open questions rather than resolved.
Character budgets are corpus-measured anchors but also zone-width-relative; the measurement method is not shown.
The file mixes audit, proposal, and implementation brief in one document.
notes: A design-DNA gap audit that turns emergent corpus patterns into concrete, derivable rule proposals while honestly cataloguing what is already covered and what still needs Tim's decision.
questions: What are the exact boundaries of the eight source collections?
How were the 207 findings bucketed and named into 28 clusters?
What does Observed against the actual faces opened this session mean without external context?
How are recurrence thresholds translated into confidence for minting new rules or tokens?
Are the live DNA key paths referenced here current, or could they have changed?
Are the proposed character budgets absolute maxima or computed relative to zone width, and how is that computed in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a staged synthesis and classification of emergent findings against live DNA, though it also reads as an implementation brief and conflict register.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the design DNA generative by converting observed patterns into derivable rules, typed tokens, and lintable constraints, while preserving human decision points for contradictions.
novelty: The explicit covered-as-principle, gap-as-concrete-rule seam; using cross-collection recurrence as rule-strength evidence; a dedicated conflict-and-open-question register for a human decision-maker; detailed computed-rule formulas; a proposal for a data-viz face.
contribution: A structured inventory of candidate rules, rationales, provenance, computed formulas, conflicts, and proposed new sections that a fused system can use to evaluate what to adopt, extend, or reconcile in the DNA without ranking one source over another.
summary_for_embedding: A machine-readable proposal synthesizing 207 emergent design findings into 28 named rule clusters, mapping each to live DNA faces as covered, new, extended, or conflicting, with computed formulas, three proposed new sections, and six open questions for Tim.
intention_for_embedding: To convert observed cross-cutting design patterns into derivable, lintable DNA rules and flag unresolved conflicts for human adjudication.
